label: disability-accommodation-request.pdf
notes: Highest-risk document type — directly blocks students with disabilities from legal entitlements

label: Spring 2022 event flyer.pdf
notes: Expired low-value content — remediating this wastes specialist time

notes: Active, time-bound, student-facing — late fees and enrollment loss are real consequences

label: university.edu/financial-aid/faq
notes: High-traffic mission-critical page; FAFSA and aid deadlines directly affect enrollment

notes: Faculty-owned content — impact depends on whether it's the only accessible version of course info

label: chemistry-dept-newsletter-spring2021.pdf
notes: Archived newsletter — no active student need, not mission-critical

label: housing-application-2025.pdf
notes: Active application form — inaccessible form fields block students from housing

label: graduation-requirements-checklist.pdf
notes: Students rely on this to plan their final semesters — errors have multi-semester consequences

notes: Policy document — student-facing but low interactivity; replace with HTML page is the right long-term call

label: football-season-recap-fall2022.pdf
notes: Time-expired promotional content — archiving is sufficient

label: university.edu/disability-services
notes: The disability services homepage being inaccessible is an acute irony and a legal priority
